data.body:
## Description
1. Add parse_passing_tests_from_output() function in record-test-status.sh mirroring parse_failing_tests_from_output: parse bash-style 'test_name ... PASS' / 'test_name: PASS' and pytest-style 'PASSED path::test_name'. Include inline comment documenting supported formats.
2. In the existing RED zone tolerance block (the all_in_red_zone == true path): after confirming all failures are in the RED zone, call parse_passing_tests_from_output on the test output, check each passing test's line number via get_test_line_number, if any is at or after red_zone_line → emit 'STALE RED MARKER: ${test_file} — RED-zone test ${passing_test} passed (line ${test_line}, RED zone starts line ${red_zone_line}); remove or update the [${red_marker}] marker' to stderr, set STATUS=failed.

## TDD Requirement
Task 3's RED tests must now pass (GREEN).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] test_stale_red_marker_partial_pass now passes (GREEN)
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'test_stale_red_marker_partial_pass.*PASS'
- [ ] test_red_tolerance_preserved_when_all_red_fail still passes
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'test_red_tolerance_preserved_when_all_red_fail.*PASS'
- [ ] Existing RED marker tolerance tests still pass
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'test_red_marker_tolerates_failure_after_marker.*PASS'
